The Vault v0.2.8 redesigns the desktop app into a premium local memory operations cockpit.

Highlights:
- Replaces the rough admin-console shell with a dark-only cockpit interface and domain-first navigation.
- Adds polished operations surfaces for Overview, Memories, Projects, Handoffs, Decisions, Loops, Graph, Recall, Analytics, Agent, and Settings.
- Adds a force-directed relationship graph using real Vault memory, project, related-item, and related-file data.
- Differentiates graph nodes by source type with project, file, and memory-specific shapes and colors.
- Reworks Open Loops into a pressure-ranked control board with filters, details, and inline actions.
- Adds an index-size status readout for the local Vault directory.
- Fixes recall trend and token-saved charts to use recall-specific telemetry.
- Replaces synthetic project radar scoring with direct counters derived from projects, memories, logs, loops, and workspaces.
- Adds renderer chunk splitting so production builds no longer emit the Vite large-chunk warning.
- Hardens release dependencies by updating Drizzle and patched MCP SDK transitive dependencies.

This build packages the desktop app and the bundled `vault-memory` MCP sidecar runtime. After installing, open The Vault and use Settings -> Client setup to connect Codex, Claude Desktop, or Claude Code.
